sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I notice the result says `revisited_state: true` and `state_node_id: 26, state_turn: 52`. This means I've returned to a state I was in before (turn 52). The white border is back at (23,37).
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. Looking at the pattern:
1. Clicking on trail segments moves the white border along the trail
2. Clicking on bordered objects (when white border is elsewhere) moves the white border there and removes another object
But I'm going in circles. Let me think about what objects have been removed so far:
- (9,49) removed after clicking (23,41)
- (48,54) removed after clicking (42,40)
The remaining gray-bordered objects are:
- (11,34)
- (23,37)
- (35,45)
- (42,40)
- (51,40)
Wait, let me re-examine. When I clicked on (42,40), (48,54) was removed. When I clicked on (23,41), (9,49) was removed. So the pattern seems to be:
- Click on object A -> white border moves to A, object B is removed
But which object B is removed? It seems to be the object that was previously white-bordered.
Let me trace the sequence:
1. Initial: white at (9,49)
2. Click (13,35): white moves to (11,34)
3. Click (37,46): white moves to (35,45)
4. Click (46,53): white moves to (48,54)
5. Click (42,40): white moves to (42,40), (48,54) removed (was white before)
6. Click (11,48): white moves to (9,49)
7. Click (23,41): white moves to (23,41), (9,49) removed (was white before)
8. Click (23,37): white moves to (23,37)
9. Click (13,35): white moves to (11,34)
10. Click (23,37): white moves to (23,37)
So the pattern is:
- Clicking on a trail segment moves the white border to the next object along the trail
- Clicking on a bordered object (when white border is elsewhere) moves the white border there AND removes the object that was previously white-bordered
So to remove objects, I need to:
1. Move white border to object A
2. Click on object B (different from A)
3. White border moves to B, and A is removed
So the strategy is to alternate between moving the white border and clicking on a different object to remove the previous one.
Currently, white border is at (23,37). To remove (23,37), I need to click on a different bordered object. Let me click on (35,45) to move the white border there and remove (23,37).
